name: baseline-passes
notes: Hand-built smoke fixture (4 files: app/models/user.rb, app/models/admin.rb, app/services/greeter.rb, app/concerns/auditable.rb, lib/runner.rb plus vendor/ and generated/) parsed cleanly by Prism 0.19.0 (Ruby 3.3.11). Oracle reports 12 TP / 0 FP / 0 FN over Class/Module/Method/Function declarations; meets gate floor P>=0.90 R>=0.75 from gates.rs.
Baseline build_phases=crawl:1ms parse:1ms decl_graph:0ms full_graph:0ms total:3ms; oracle_ms=74. No oracle-level FP/FN to repair.

name: query-surface-cross-file-dispatch
notes: Closed three smoke-query gaps without touching the symbol oracle: (1) extractor now emits a Field ParsedReference for explicit-receiver calls (`user.full_name`) mirroring Python's `attribute` handling, so `references_to_symbol(User#full_name)` surfaces the dotted form; (2) graph resolver falls back to a receiver-name -> class heuristic (`user` -> `User`) inside `inherited_ruby_method` and a new `ruby_property_reference_matches` helper hooked into `reference_binding_confidence`, giving cross-file `promote -> full_name` a Heuristic-confidence call edge; (3) `require_relative`/`require`/`load`/`autoload` directives now synthesize a Function symbol whose signature is the raw source text, surfaced via `signature_search` and excluded from the oracle symbol scan by the new `ruby:import-directive` attribute.

Oracle stays at 12 TP / 0 FP / 0 FN, P=R=1.0 after the new `RubyImportDirective` exclusion. All 10 smoke queries pass; `ruby-references-to-symbol`, `ruby-call-chain-cross-file`, and `ruby-import-resolution` were the previously-failing trio.
